name: Your First Battle
description: Learn the basics of movement, recruiting, supply points, time of day, combat, and leveling up against a CPU opponent.

guides.title: Welcome, Commander!
guides.text: You win by defeating the enemy leader (★); you lose if your own leader falls. Start by tapping one of your units, then pick a destination from the hexes highlighted in blue. The path and remaining movement are previewed before you move.

guides.title: Recruiting Units
guides.text: While your leader (★) is standing on the flag (🚩), tap the leader and then tap the flag again to recruit reinforcements (they're placed on a camp hex). Recruited units can't act the turn they arrive. Your current supplies and per-turn income are shown at the top of the screen. Note: the flag is just where reinforcements arrive — capturing it does not win the game (only defeating the enemy leader does).

guides.title: Securing Supply Points
guides.text: Stopping on a supply point (the tent tile) claims it and ends that unit's move for the turn. Each point you hold gives +2 supplies per turn, heals 8 HP and cures poison for any unit standing on it at the start of your turn, and reduces your upkeep (the total level of your non-leader units) by one per point held. Securing your supply line is the key to this game's economy.

guides.title: Ending Your Turn & Time of Day
guides.text: Once everyone has acted, use the "⋯" menu in the bottom right and choose "End Turn". Keep an eye on the time of day at the top of the screen: lawful units (most Loyalists) deal +25% damage by day, while chaotic units (like the undead) deal +25% by night. Pushing forward at night can get you badly punished.

guides.title: Combat Tips
guides.text: Tap an enemy unit adjacent to one of yours to preview the hit chance and expected damage. Hit chance depends on the terrain the defender is standing on (forests and hills are hard to hit). When attacking, target enemies on open ground; when defending, stand on strong terrain yourself. Retaliation only happens with an attack of the same range (melee/ranged).

guides.title: Experience & Leveling Up
guides.text: Fighting earns experience (the purple bar under a unit); filling it levels the unit up and fully heals it. Finishing off an enemy grants a large bonus (8 × the enemy's level). Units with more than one advancement option let you choose. Try not to throw away units you've invested in.
